project.name: Claim to Commit
project.description: A local evidence workbench that audits whether software claims are backed by decisions, commits, changed files, tests, and visual proof.

claims.title: Claims are graded by transparent deterministic rules
claims.description: The same repository revision and manifest always produce the same proven, partial, or unsupported result.

evidence.label: Public scoring rules
evidence.detail: DEC-003 is recorded in DECISIONS.md.

evidence.detail: Codex and GPT-5.6 built the product contract, strict scaffold, evidence schema, Git adapters, deterministic audit engine, and scan API in this continuous session.

claims.title: Automatically infers evidence relationships with perfect accuracy
claims.description: A deliberately unsupported demonstration claim. Claim to Commit verifies explicit links; it does not pretend semantic guesses are proof.

evidence.label: No implementation exists
evidence.detail: Commit 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000 could not be resolved in the scanned history.

findings.message: Commit 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000 could not be resolved in the scanned history.
findings.guidance: Reference an implementation commit that exists in this repository.

findings.message: No implementation commit proves this claim.
findings.guidance: Connect the claim to a resolvable implementation commit before presenting it as delivered.

findings.message: No resolved file evidence supports this claim.
findings.guidance: Reference a changed implementation file that exists in the repository.

findings.message: No resolved test evidence supports this claim.
findings.guidance: Declare a machine-readable passing test result for this claim.

formula: 9 proven weight ÷ 9 scored weight × 100 · 1 audit control excluded
